blocks.content: Ethan was still near the door when Cassandra turned to find him, and the particular thing that happened when their eyes met was the thing it had been for months — a specific quality of recognition, of being seen by someone who was actually looking. He had been trying to draw it and had not fully succeeded. He thought he might never fully succeed. Some things resisted the page.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Hi.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Hi.

That was all, and it was enough. She came over to him and he put his arm around her briefly, just a moment, her shoulder against his side — and then they separated because there were other people in the room and the night was just beginning, but the contact had been sufficient.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You look —

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Don't.
But she was smiling.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I was going to say you look like someone who's been outside.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: It was mild out. We walked.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: That's all I was going to say.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: No it wasn't.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You're right. It wasn't.
She laughed. The particular laugh — the unguarded one, the one he'd been paying attention to since before he'd known he was paying attention.

Maya arrived seven minutes later. She came in with the quality she'd had in all their encounters — contained, aware, not unfriendly. But something had shifted since the family dinner. The bracing was gone. She looked at the room, looked at Ethan, and gave him a nod that was brief and genuine.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Nice place.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: Thank you. You're here. That makes it better.
She looked at him with a slight surprise that she quickly managed.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You're very deliberate, aren't you.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: I've had years to practice.
He handed her a cup of tea.
